description: Users report multi-line -m/--message is awkward because it forces shell heredoc gymnastics ($(cat <<'EOF' ...)), so they default to terse one-line commit messages and lose the 50/72 body. Adopt git's convention: make -m repeatable on bl review and bl close; the values are joined with blank lines (first = title, rest = body paragraphs). Single -m keeps working identically. Why: the friction is purely shell-quoting; matching 'git commit -m a -m b' removes it with zero new concepts and no behavior change for existing callers.
title: Repeatable -m for review/close: build multi-line messages without heredocs
